content:
# Bandera Formation

**Summary**: A sandstone formation used to evaluate the effects of acidizing and chelating agents.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2016-hamza|HAMZA, 2016]] used Bandera sandstone cores to test new chemical formulations.

- [[2016-jad|Jad, 2016]] investigated the damage of Bandera Brown sandstone core samples.

- [[2021-almarri|AlMarri, 2021]] used Bandera Gray Sandstone samples to evaluate the performance of flue gas injection.

## Depositional Environment



- [[2016-jad|Jad, 2016]] investigated the damage of Bandera Brown sandstone core samples.

- [[2021-almarri|AlMarri, 2021]] used Bandera Gray Sandstone samples to evaluate the performance of flue gas injection.

## Reservoir Properties

- [[2016-hamza|HAMZA, 2016]] porosity and permeability measured during core flood

## Diagenetic History



## Key Findings Across Sources

- Bandera cores showed a 40% enhancement in permeability when using seawater-based fluids. ([[2016-hamza|HAMZA, 2016, p. 19]])

- Bandera Brown sample BB4 showed an increase in porosity after HCl treatment due to clay dissolution. ([[2016-jad|Jad, 2016, p. 89]])

- The Bandera gray had a wettability index of +0.35 (slightly water wet). ([[2021-almarri|AlMarri, 2021, p. 51]])
